prompt: Macro photograph of a tiny glass cephalopod baby nestled in a cupped adult tentacle. The adult tentacle is warm amber and glowing, its sucker visible. Cradled in the curl of the tentacle sits a 15mm hatchling, entirely CLEAR and GLASSY, its skin shimmering with COOL IRIDESCENT STRUCTURAL COLOR — opal blues, greens, silver-violet — bright against the warm amber parent. The baby has TWO LONG THIN SNAIL-LIKE EYE STALKS, comically oversized, rising from its head. Eight fine tentacles. The contrast: warm amber adult, cool iridescent child. Deep black background, macro detail, otherworldly.
